title: agrostis tenuis f. umbrosa
intro: Agrostis tenuis f. umbrosa, also known as red fescue, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is valued for its ability to soothe digestive issues and relieve stress. The health benefits of red fescue include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve sleep quality, and reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. Herbal preparations of Agrostis tenuis f. umbrosa typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications.
